mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP compresses equity risk premiums by confirming corporate earnings expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in positive GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5 percent signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenues, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.5%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicate synchronized real-economy expansion, which reduces discount rates and lifts equity valuations over a 60-day horizon through reinforced earnings momentum.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero information coefficient over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns fail to exceed the unconditional mean by at least 0.5% in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with accelerating core inflation compress equity multiples by increasing the discount rate and signaling policy-induced demand destruction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand or remain stable despite simultaneous rate hikes and inflation acceleration, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain efficiency, which supports corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth consistently correlates with negative or flat equity returns over a 2-year period, or the signal's hit rate falls below 48% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real personal savings exceeding 2 percent signals household balance sheet stress and impending consumption contraction, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 3% over the 20-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market cycle that sustains wage-driven consumption, lifting equity valuations over a 60-day horizon through durable earnings growth.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces a negative information ratio over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in confirmed labor market tightening periods do not statistically differ from flat market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent quarterly GDP contraction paired with persistent inflation above 3 percent indicates stagflationary pressure that erodes real corporate cash flows and forces multiple compression, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equities rally or hold value despite simultaneous GDP contraction and elevated inflation, or the interaction effect reverses sign and becomes statistically insignificant over a 24-month walk-forward test.
